title: Virginia Natural Gas and HRSD Launch $31 Million RNG Project in Virginia Beach
content: Virginia Natural Gas (VNG) and HRSD have announced a $31 million partnership to construct a Renewable Natural Gas (RNG) facility at the Atlantic Treatment Plant in Virginia Beach. Recently approved by the Virginia State Corporation Commission, the project will capture biogas generated from wastewater treatmentâ€”which is currently flaredâ€”and upgrade it into pipeline-quality natural gas. The facility is expected to produce over 200,000 MMBtu of RNG annually, enough to heat approximately 5,000 homes. Construction is scheduled to begin later in 2026, with the plant becoming operational in 2027. This initiative aligns with Virginiaâ€™s Energy Innovation Act of 2022, aimed at reducing emissions and utilizing local resources. VNG plans to sell the associated environmental credits to third parties to help maintain affordability while preserving community benefits.
author: Analysts Account
